claims: LLMs can significantly augment or substitute real-world datasets in data-scarce scenarios
Synthetic data improves model performance in low-resource tasks by 3–26%
LLM-generated code can be validated for functional correctness through execution
Synthetic data generation reduces annotation costs and enables data augmentation
Retrieval-augmented pipelines improve factual accuracy in generated text
Iterative self-training on synthetic data risks model collapse
Filtering and weighting synthetic examples improve data quality
Reinforcement learning with execution feedback enhances code synthesis reliability
limitations: Generated text may contain factual inaccuracies
Stylistic and distributional realism of synthetic data is often insufficient
Risk of bias amplification in LLM-generated data
Potential for model collapse with iterative self-training
Challenges in maintaining diversity while preserving data fidelity
